Title 8 — Health & Safety Plain-Language Service Free · no account

A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 8 (Health & Safety) — the County's nuisance, sanitation, noise, and solid-waste rules, and how violations are enforced. Solid waste is run by the Department of Environmental Management. This is a structural first version — much of Title 8 is expanding as the ingestion corpus is sourced verbatim; we assert only what is cited; nothing here is legal advice.

★ Parcel lookup live data

Many health-and-safety matters (overgrown lots, illegal dumping, sanitation nuisances) are tied to a property. Enter a Maui County TMK to see the parcel's designations from the Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS as context before reporting or responding to a nuisance.

Tip: type the digits of your TMK (zone-section-plat-parcel). Don't know it? Open the State Land Use Viewer ↗ to find it on a map.

Live designations: Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS. No parcel data is stored or fabricated — fetched live. nuisance / code-case overlay

1 · What Title 8 covers expanding

A structural overview while chapters are sourced verbatim. We name only what is in the Code's own structure.

Title 8 (Health & Safety, §§8.04.005–8.34.080) carries the County's public-health, sanitation, nuisance, noise, and solid-waste rules. Solid waste — collection, transfer stations, the landfill, and illegal-dumping rules — is administered by the Department of Environmental Management, Solid Waste Division. The chapter-by-chapter content is expanding as the ingestion session sources each chapter from Municode; until then, use the Municode link for authority.

Maui County Code Title 8 (Health & Safety) + County Solid Waste Division. per-chapter content (nuisance, noise, sanitation, solid waste)

2 · Common Title 8 matters &amp; where to go expanding

Where residents typically interact with Title 8.

MatterWhat it coversWhere
Trash, recycling, transfer stations, landfillSolid-waste collection & disposalSolid Waste ↗
Illegal dumpingDumping on public/private landreport ↗
NoiseCounty noise rules (+ State noise code)Title 8 ↗
Sanitation nuisanceConditions injurious to healthTitle 8 ↗

County of Maui Solid Waste Division + MCC Title 8. exact reporting channels per matter

3 · Enforcement sourced

Health-and-safety violations are reachable through the County enforcement engine. Not legal advice.

Title 19's enforcement chapter, §19.530.030, expressly reaches violations of MCC titles 8, 12, 14, 16, 18, 19 and 20 plus related rules and permits — so a Title 8 health-and-safety violation (e.g. an unsanitary nuisance or illegal dumping) can draw a notice of violation and order requiring abatement and/or civil fines, final after 30 days unless appealed to the Board of Variances and Appeals.

Maui County Code Ch. 19.530 (§19.530.030 reach, sourced in the Title 19 service). Note: the §15-2/MC-15 cross-title fine schedule is specific to Titles 16·18·19 and does not extend to Title 8. Title 8-specific penalty provisions

4 · Who pays — the charter lens analysis

Labeled analysis — health & safety read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.

Maui County Charter — lensUnder the Maui County Charter, the County protects public health and safety through sanitation, nuisance, and solid-waste programs administered by Environmental Management; fees (e.g. tipping fees) and the general fund support the service.
Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) — lensUnder the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, a clean, healthy living environment is a shared birthright; protecting it is a communal kuleana. The lens favors accessible disposal and strong action against polluters, and resists fees so high they push residents toward illegal dumping.
⚖️ Position — analysis, not lawKeep legal disposal easy and affordable so the clean path is the cheap path, and aim enforcement at those who dump on the commons. Same title, read two ways.
Free now (no account): this page, the parcel lookup, and the structural overview are all Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a nuisance-report helper and an AI Title-8 assistant will be verification-gated via the free govOS beta portal (identity verified with Stripe Identity — free, no charge); only verified-status is stored, never the raw ID. No secret keys (environment references only).
